title: Indexes: The Array Address 🏠
description: Learn how Java accesses items inside an array.

slides.title: The Zero Index 0️⃣
slides.content:
So you have an array with 5 scores in it.
How do you get one of the scores out? You use its **Address**, mathematically called an **Index**.

But Java (and almost all programming languages) counts starting from `0`, not `1`!

slides.title: Accessing 🏠
slides.content:
To get a value, you type the Array's name, followed by Square Brackets `[]` with the Index inside.

`String firstItem = backpack[0];`

quizData.question: What does `backpack[1]` equip in the code above?
quizData.explanation: Counting starts at 0. So index 0 is Sword, index 1 is Shield.

slides.title: Out of Bounds 🚧
slides.content:
## Array Index Out Of Bounds Exception

This is the most famous error in Java. It happens when you ask for an address that doesn't exist.

If your array has 3 items, the addresses are 0, 1, and 2. 
If you ask for `backpack[3]`, Java will crash! There is no item at index 3.

quizData.question: If an Array holds 10 items, what is the address of the VERY LAST item?
quizData.explanation: Because counting starts at 0, the items go from index 0 to index 9. Asking for index 10 will crash the app!
